textPlain: Can't wait for my code being used as training data for Amazon to make a profit

Is it just me or the new Amazon/AWS is giving off some serious early-Satya Nadella Microsoft vibes. Kiro, Q....Think back to when they (MSOFT) were putting out tools like TypeScript, WSL, and VS Code. It looks like Amazon is doing something similar now, building cool tech for developers without plastering the AWS brand all over it.
Where do you see that the data is being used as such?
Your thesis is valid until "without plastering the AWS brand all over it." Lol
It is literally called amazonq.nvim and it makes it so some Amazon service is usable with NeoVIM.
